title: Residualized Undercut Opportunity Features
summary: F1 strategy papers emphasize the trade-off between tyre degradation, pit-loss, and traffic/competition.
rationale: F1 strategy papers emphasize the trade-off between tyre degradation, pit-loss, and traffic/competition. Rather than raw LapTime_Delta and degradation alone, model the expected lap-time baseline first and use residual pace loss, residual trend, and relative field position as explicit undercut/overcut opportunity features.
implementation_hint: Fit a fold-local regression or state-space baseline for LapTime or LapTime_Delta from Compound, TyreLife, RaceProgress, LapNumber, and race identity. Add features like current residual, 2-3 lap residual slope, residual percentile within same race-lap, and estimated gain from a tyre reset relative to peers.
expected_effect: More physically grounded features may isolate the true trigger for pitting and reduce noise from track-specific pace levels.
risk: Two-stage modeling adds complexity and can leak if the baseline is fit on all rows; residual estimates may be unstable in wet or highly anomalous laps.
